[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)] ⭐ Run Main : parse toolchain version
if [[ -z $toolchain ]]; then
  # GitHub does not enforce `required: true` inputs itself. https://github.com/actions/runner/issues/1070
  echo "'toolchain' is a required input" >&2
  exit 1
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' '[0-9]+' '(year|month|week|day)s?' 'ago$ ]]; then
  if [[ Linux == macOS ]]; then
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date -v-$(sed 's/stable \([0-9]*\) \(.\).*/\1\2/' <<< $toolchain) +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  else
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date --date "${toolchain#stable }" +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  fi
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' 'minus' '[0-9]+' 'releases?$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6-${toolchain//[^0-9]/}))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^1\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((i=${toolchain#1.}, c=($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6, i+9*i*(10*i<=c)+90*i*(100*i<=c)))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
else
  echo "toolchain=$toolchain" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
fi

[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)] ⭐ Run Main : install rustup if needed
if ! command -v rustup &>/dev/null; then
  curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 --retry 10 --retry-connrefused --location --silent --show-error --fail https://sh.rustup.rs | sh -s -- --default-toolchain none -y
  echo "$CARGO_HOME/bin" >> $GITHUB_PATH
fi

[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)] ⭐ Run Main : enable Cargo sparse registry
# implemented in 1.66, stabilized in 1.68, made default in 1.70
if [ -z "${CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL+set}" -o -f "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol ]; then
  if rustc +stable --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[89]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=sparse >> $GITHUB_ENV
  elif rustc +stable --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[67]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=git >> $GITHUB_ENV
  fi
fi

[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   | 
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   | Pros & Cons
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   | 
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   |   [1] Ollama
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   |     + Easiest setup — just `ollama pull phi3:mini`
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   |     + Supports hundreds of models (llama3, mistral, gemma2, qwen2…)
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   |     + Auto GPU/CPU fallback, model management built-in
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   |     − Requires Ollama daemon running alongside NTK (two processes)
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   |     − External installation: https://ollama.ai
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   | 
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   |   [2] Candle  (in-process inference)
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   |     + Single binary, no external processes
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   |     + Direct CUDA/Metal/CPU access, lowest overhead
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   |     + Works offline once GGUF + tokenizer.json downloaded
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   |     − GGUF + tokenizer.json must be downloaded (~2.2 GB)
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   |     − Limited to GGUF-format models (Phi-3, Llama, Mistral…)
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   | 
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   |   [3] llama.cpp
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   |     + Best CPU performance (AVX2 / AMX optimisations)
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   |     + Excellent CUDA/Metal GPU support
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   |     + Works offline once GGUF downloaded (~2.2 GB)
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   |     − Requires llama-server: brew install llama.cpp  or GitHub releases
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   |     − Extra process to manage (auto-started by NTK daemon)
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   | 
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   |   💡  Recommendation: [2] Candle
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   | 
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   | Choose backend [1/2/3] or Enter for [2]: 
[CI/Test (ubuntu-latest)]   | [2] Candle — In-process inference
